trial_label: A Phase III Randomized Trial of Metformin vs Placebo in Earl

rubric.target_patient: A newly diagnosed (within 12 months of first histologic diagnosis) early-stage invasive breast cancer patient, adjuvant or neoadjuvant, with early-stage pT/pN combinations (e.g., pT1c pN0 triple-negative, or node-limited disease undergoing breast-conserving surgery with sentinel node biopsy), who has undergone axillary staging and is being considered for a 5-year systemic therapy trial layered on standard care. This is a general early-stage breast cancer population, not a niche molecular subtype, seen in routine breast oncology practice.
rubric.site_burden: This is a long-duration (5-year treatment, with extended follow-up) placebo-controlled oral drug trial requiring randomization, blinded drug dispensation with formal pill counts at each dispensation, compliance tracking, and mandatory tumour block banking/shipment to a central tissue bank (mandatory for site participation even though patient tissue donation is optional). The posted schedule-of-assessments page was heading-only and not a structured visit grid, so exact visit cadence cannot be confirmed from the record; burden here is inferred from discontinuation/follow-up and tissue-banking sections rather than a stated visit schedule. Sites must sustain long-term follow-up even after subjects discontinue treatment or leave the institution, implying durable patient retention infrastructure and multi-year study coordination capacity.

criteria.requirement: Investigator must be a breast cancer treating specialist (medical oncology, surgical oncology, or hematology-oncology with a breast focus) capable of managing adjuvant/neoadjuvant systemic therapy decisions.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists Hematology & Oncology, Medical Oncology, or Surgical Oncology, or self-report confirms a breast cancer-focused oncology practice.
criteria.derived_from: Population: invasive breast cancer, adjuvant/neoadjuvant systemic therapy staging (pT/pN eligibility) requiring oncologic management

criteria.requirement: The site must have a steady volume of newly diagnosed, early-stage invasive breast cancer patients presenting within 12 months of diagnosis, since eligibility requires enrollment in that narrow post-diagnosis window.
criteria.satisfies_if: Medicare claims data or self-report show recurring breast cancer diagnosis/treatment volume consistent with a dedicated breast oncology practice (e.g., multiple new invasive breast cancer patients per year).
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: enrollment within 12 months after first histologic diagnosis of invasive breast cancer

criteria.requirement: Site must have access to sentinel lymph node biopsy and/or axillary lymph node dissection services, either in-house or via tightly coordinated referral, since all subjects require this staging.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms surgical oncology/breast surgery partnership or in-house capability for SLNB/ALND at the enrolling site or affiliated institution.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: All subjects must have sentinel lymph node biopsy and/or axillary lymph node dissection

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to obtain or coordinate baseline staging imaging (chest X-ray/CT chest, bone scan, abdominal imaging) prior to randomization.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms on-site or readily accessible imaging services (radiology/nuclear medicine) for staging workup.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: staging investigations (chest imaging, bone scan, abdominal imaging) performed between diagnosis and randomization

criteria.requirement: Site must have infrastructure to maintain patient follow-up and blinded drug dispensation with pill-count compliance tracking over a 5-year treatment period, including after treatment discontinuation.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms dedicated research coordinator/pharmacy support for long-duration blinded drug trials with compliance monitoring and extended follow-up.
criteria.derived_from: Section 12: pill counts, compliance tracking, and continued follow-up after treatment discontinuation over the 5-year intervention

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to identify, retrieve, and ship diagnostic tumour blocks to the central CCTG tissue bank, as this is mandatory for site participation.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms pathology department cooperation and established process for tumour block retrieval/shipment for research use.
criteria.derived_from: Section 13.2: tissue collection mandatory for site participation, tumour blocks banked at Queen's University

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience serving as an investigator on breast cancer or oncology clinical trials, given the complexity of a long-term blinded randomized cooperative-group trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician as an investigator (PI or sub-I) on at least one completed breast cancer or oncology interventional trial.
criteria.derived_from: Trial sponsored by Canadian Cancer Trials Group (cooperative group network trial), Phase III randomized, 344 sites

criteria.requirement: Investigator/site should have an existing relationship with a cooperative oncology trials network (e.g., NCIC CTG/CCTG or affiliated US cooperative groups) given this is a network-sponsored trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov or self-report shows the site/investigator participated in other CCTG- or NCI cooperative group-sponsored trials.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: Canadian Cancer Trials Group (NETWORK), 344 sites including 280 US sites

criteria.requirement: Some history of receiving research-related payments (a proxy for active clinical trial conduct) supports capacity to run a multi-year randomized drug trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments record shows research payments to the physician or affiliated institution in oncology-related research categories.
criteria.derived_from: Phase III randomized multi-site drug trial requiring sustained trial infrastructure

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have experience running blinded, placebo-controlled drug trials with unblinding procedures for medical emergencies, given the blinding-integrity requirements described in the protocol.
criteria.satisfies_if: Self-report confirms prior experience as investigator on a blinded placebo-controlled trial with documented unblinding procedures.
criteria.derived_from: Section 12.2: blinding critical to integrity, procedures for breaking blind in emergencies

excluded_screening: Histologic confirmation of invasive breast cancer and specific pT/pN eligibility combinations — verified per patient at enrollment, not a site characteristic
Bilateral mammogram within 12 months / physical exam of chest wall — per-patient imaging requirement, not physician-fit
Hematology and biochemistry lab values (WBC, platelets, AST/ALT, creatinine, bilirubin) — per-patient lab thresholds for drug safety, unrelated to site capability
ECOG Performance Status 0-2 — per-patient functional status check at baseline
Age 18 to <75 and life expectancy ≥5 years — per-patient eligibility bound
Ability and willingness to complete QOL questionnaires in English/French — per-patient consent/compliance item, sub-study now closed
Ability and willingness to complete physical activity/diet questionnaires — per-patient sub-study consent item, now closed
Pregnancy/lactation status and contraception use — per-patient safety screening for drug exposure

Staging investigations (bone scan, abdominal imaging) required only if signs/symptoms present — conditional per-patient workup, not a site infrastructure requirement per se beyond general imaging access already captured
